title: Calibration in Safety-Critical Tasks
concept: In safety-critical domains, calibration is essential for risk management, decision thresholding, and human-AI handoffs.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What chart do we use to visualize model calibration performance?
exercises.explain: Correct! Reliability diagrams plot confidence vs. accuracy to expose calibration gaps.

cards.title: Knowing when to ask for help
cards.body: Imagine an AI assistant in a hospital. If it is 99% confident in a tumor diagnosis, the doctor might proceed. If it is 50% confident, the doctor must check the scan manually. If the AI is overconfident and wrong, patients suffer. Calibration is a safety shield.

cards.title: Safety Calibration
cards.body: In safety-critical tasks (autonomous driving, medical imaging, financial transactions), models use calibration to manage risk. A calibrated model allows engineers to set reliable decision thresholds and trigger human interventions when confidence is low.

cards.title: A pressure relief valve
cards.body: Think of calibration as a pressure relief valve on a steam engine. If pressure gets too high (uncertainty is high), the valve opens to release steam (hands control to a human). If the valve's sensor is miscalibrated, the engine might explode.

cards.title: Robotic surgery safety
cards.body: A surgical robot uses CV to identify blood vessels. The system is programmed to cut only if P(No_vessel) >= 0.999. If the model is calibrated, the risk of cutting a vessel is 1 in 1000. If uncalibrated, that risk might jump to 1 in 10, which is unacceptable.

cards.title: Accuracy is not enough for safety
cards.body: You might think a model with 99% accuracy is safe. But if that 1% error rate contains highly confident mistakes (e.g. classifying a pedestrian as empty road with 99.9% confidence), the system is dangerous. A calibrated model would flag that pedestrian with low confidence.

left.text:
Uncertain sample -> low confidence
Triggers human check
System safety is maintained

right.text:
Uncertain sample -> high confidence
Bypasses safety checks
Failure occurs in production

exercises.prompt: Why is calibration highly valued in medical and autonomous vehicle AI applications?
exercises.explain: Calibrated confidence scores let engineers set dependable thresholds to trigger human handoffs when the model is uncertain.

options.text: It allows the system to reliably flag uncertain predictions for human review.
It makes the model run ten times faster.
It eliminates all errors from the model.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In safety-critical applications, a false positive with 99.9% confidence is much worse than a false positive with 51% confidence.
exercises.explain: True. A 99.9% confident error is hard to catch with safety thresholds and will bypass human review, causing high risk.
Risk management is the core purpose of setting confidence thresholds in production.
exercises.sentence: Safety thresholds use calibrated probabilities to manage operational ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems demonstrates correct safety calibration design?
exercises.explain: Recognizing moderate confidence (P=0.45) and taking a safe, defensive action demonstrates good safety design.

options.text: A vehicle model that flags an obstacle as P=0.45, prompting the system to slow down and verify
A model that outputs P=0.99 for all objects, forcing the car to swerve constantly
A model that ignores all confidence scores and drives by raw logits

exercises.prompt: You are a lead AI architect at a bank. You are deploying a loan fraud detection model. The model must flag transactions with high fraud probability for immediate blocking. How do you set the policy?

options.text: Apply temperature scaling to calibrate logits on a validation set, then block transactions where P(Fraud) >= 0.90.
Block any transaction where the raw logit is positive.
Retrain the model on random weights daily to randomize the threshold.
Do not calibrate, just block if the model outputs any confidence score above 0.50.

exercises.explain: Exactly! First, you ensure the confidence scores are calibrated using temperature scaling, so '0.90' actually means a 90% chance of fraud. Then, you can apply your business threshold confidently, minimizing false blocks for legitimate users. Fantastic job completing the Confidence Calibration unit!
